note: AUTHORED reconstruction, NOT survey data. An approximate polyline tracing the 1748 northern Thiên Lý / Mandarin Road branch as it threaded through what is now Bình Thạnh — Thị Nghè → Cầu Sơn → Cầu Kinh → the Thanh Đa / Bình Quới ferry. Coordinates are hand-placed along the known corridor (Xô Viết Nghệ Tĩnh + đường Bình Quới) for cartographic illustration; they are a scholarly reconstruction per Phạm Hoàng Quân / Nguyễn Đình Đầu, not a surviving physical marker or a GPS trace.

crossings.name: Cầu Thị Nghè
crossings.note: rạch Thị Nghè crossing — the route's southern gate into the district

crossings.name: Bến đò Bình Quới
crossings.note: the Bình Quới / Thanh Đa ferry — the route's far end

crossings.name: Cầu Bình Lợi (1902)
crossings.note: first bridge across the Saigon River, 1902 — a later crossing near the corridor

water_hint: rạch Thị Nghè runs along the southern edge; the Saigon River wraps the eastern and northern (Thanh Đa) peninsula
